tasks.title: Fix friend request API endpoint mismatch and add friends list call
tasks.description:
CRITICAL BUG: The mobile app sends friend requests to POST /feels/friends but the backend route is POST /feels/friends/add. This causes all friend requests to fail silently.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/friends.tsx

Fix 1 — Line 54: Change `api.post('/feels/friends', { friend_email: friendEmail.trim() })` to `api.post('/feels/friends/add', { friend_email: friendEmail.trim() })`.

Fix 2 — The friends list only fetches today's friend feels via GET /feels/friends (GetFriendFeels handler). This means friends who haven't checked in today are invisible. The app should ALSO call GET /feels/friends/list to get the complete accepted friends list, then merge with today's feels data.

In loadData(), add a fourth API call: `api.get('/feels/friends/list')`. Create a new state variable `allFriends` for the full list. In the friends list section, display allFriends instead of just friends (today's feels). For each friend in allFriends, check if they exist in the friends array (today's feels) to show their current score, otherwise show a gray 'No check-in today' state.

Fix 3 — Guest mode counter: In mobile/contexts/AuthContext.tsx, the guest_uses counter is never incremented when a guest performs a check-in. In mobile/app/(protected)/home.tsx handleCheckIn function, after successful check-in, add guest usage tracking: if isGuest, read guest_uses from SecureStore, increment by 1, save back. If guest_uses >= 3, show an Alert prompting to sign up and redirect to login.

Test command verifies TypeScript compilation after all fixes.

tasks.title: Enhance content moderation filter with comprehensive word list and scoring
tasks.description:
The current content moderation filter in backend/internal/services/moderation_service.go only checks for two words ('spam' and 'scam') using a basic regex. This is inadequate for App Store compliance (Guideline 1.2 — User Generated Content).

File: backend/internal/services/moderation_service.go

Replace the basic prohibitedPatterns with a comprehensive moderation system:

1. Expand the prohibited patterns list to include common profanity, slurs, and harmful content patterns. Create categories: profanity (20+ patterns), harassment (10+ patterns), self-harm keywords (5+ patterns), spam indicators (URLs, repeated chars).

2. Add a scoring system: each matched pattern has a severity weight (1-10). If total score > 5, content is flagged. This prevents false positives on borderline words while catching truly harmful content.

3. Add SanitizeContent enhancement: replace matched words with asterisks of the same length instead of generic '[filtered]'.

4. Add FilterNote function specifically for check-in notes (called from feel_service.go CreateFeelCheck before saving): `func (s *ModerationService) FilterNote(text string) (sanitized string, flagged bool)`. In feel_service.go, inject ModerationService and call FilterNote on the note field before database insert.

File: backend/internal/services/feel_service.go — Add moderationService field to FeelService struct. Update NewFeelService constructor to accept *ModerationService. In CreateFeelCheck, before creating the record: `sanitized, flagged := s.moderationService.FilterNote(req.Note); req.Note = sanitized`.

File: backend/cmd/server/main.go — Pass moderationService to NewFeelService constructor.

This creates a production-grade content filter that protects users while minimizing false positives.

tasks.title: Add personalized AI-generated insight messages using real mood data analysis
tasks.description:
The backend weekly insights endpoint returns a basic message field but the message generation logic in feel_service.go GetWeeklyInsights is simplistic. Enhance it with data-driven personalized messages.

File: backend/internal/services/feel_service.go — In GetWeeklyInsights, replace the simple message generation with a comprehensive analysis engine:

1. Create a new function `generatePersonalizedMessage(current, previous WeeklyInsight, improvement float64) string` that analyzes multiple dimensions:
   - Trend direction (improving/declining/stable) with magnitude
   - Streak consistency (how many days checked in)
   - Mood-energy gap (if mood is much higher than energy or vice versa)
   - Best day patterns (is their best day always a weekend?)
   - Emoji usage patterns (dominant emoji sentiment)

2. Message templates (20+ variations) categorized by scenario:
   - Thriving (score 80+, improving): "You're on fire this week! Your mood has been climbing steadily. Keep up whatever you're doing!"
   - Good but declining: "Your scores are solid but trending down slightly. Consider what made {bestDay} feel so great and replicate it."
   - Low energy high mood: "Your mood is good but energy is lagging. Try getting more sleep or adding a short walk to your routine."
   - Consistent checker: "7 days straight — your consistency is impressive! Regular check-ins build self-awareness."
   - Recovering (was low, now improving): "You're bouncing back! Your scores improved by {improvement}% this week. That takes real resilience."
   - Weekend warrior: "Interesting pattern — your best days are always on weekends. What changes could make weekdays feel more like {bestDay}?"

3. Select message based on weighted scoring of all factors. Never repeat the same template two weeks in a row (track last message template index in FeelStreak model — add LastMessageIdx int field).

File: backend/internal/models/feel_check.go — Add `LastMessageIdx int` field to FeelStreak model.

This transforms generic insights into genuinely useful, personalized feedback that increases user retention.

tasks.title: Add monthly mood calendar heatmap to insights screen
tasks.description:
Add a visual monthly calendar heatmap showing mood scores by color intensity for each day of the current month. This gives users an at-a-glance view of their monthly patterns.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/insights.tsx

1. Add new API call in loadInsights: `const historyRes = await api.get('/feels/history?limit=31&offset=0')` to get last 31 days of check-ins.

2. Add state: `const [monthData, setMonthData] = useState<FeelCheck[]>([])`. Store history response in monthData.

3. Create helper function `buildCalendarGrid(checks: FeelCheck[]): (FeelCheck | null)[][]` that:
   - Gets current month's first day and number of days
   - Creates a 2D array of weeks (rows) x 7 days (columns)
   - Maps check-ins to their respective day cells by matching check_date
   - Empty days before month start and after month end are null

4. Add calendar UI section after the personalized message card:
   - Section header: 'Monthly Overview' with month name (e.g., 'February 2026')
   - Day labels row: S M T W T F S in text-xs text-gray-400
   - Grid of 5-6 rows, 7 columns using nested View with flex-row
   - Each cell: 36x36 rounded-lg View. If check-in exists, background color = `getColorForScore(check.feel_score) + '40'` (40% opacity), with the score number in text-xs. If no check-in, bg-gray-100. If null (outside month), transparent.
   - Today's cell gets a 2px border in primary-600

5. Import FeelCheck and getColorForScore from '../../types/feel'.

6. Add pull-to-refresh to reload both insights and history data.

Styling: The calendar grid should be inside a rounded-2xl bg-white p-4 shadow-sm card. Each cell has 2px gap. Day labels use font-semibold. The active month name uses text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900.

tasks.title: Add profile screen with avatar, stats summary, and badge showcase
tasks.description:
Currently there is no dedicated profile screen. Users can only see their email in settings. Add a Profile tab that replaces the current Settings tab position, moving settings to a gear icon in the profile header.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/profile.tsx — Create new screen:

Imports: React, useState, useEffect, View, Text, ScrollView, Pressable, ActivityIndicator from react-native, SafeAreaView, useAuth, useRouter, api, haptics, getColorForScore, getFeelLabel, FeelStats, Ionicons.

State: stats (FeelStats), isLoading (boolean).

useEffect: Call GET /feels/stats to load user stats.

UI Structure (SafeAreaView bg-gray-50):
1. Header row: 'Profile' text-3xl font-bold + settings gear icon (Pressable with Ionicons 'settings-outline') that navigates to /(protected)/settings.

2. Avatar section (items-center mt-6): Large 96x96 rounded-full bg-primary-100 circle with first letter of email in text-4xl font-bold text-primary-600. Below: email in text-base text-gray-600.

3. Stats grid (3 columns, mt-6, flex-row gap-3 px-6): Current streak (fire emoji + number + 'Day Streak'), Total check-ins (chart emoji + number + 'Check-ins'), Average score (sparkle emoji + number + 'Avg Score'). Each in rounded-2xl bg-white p-4 shadow-sm items-center.

4. Badge showcase section (mt-6 px-6): 'Your Badges' header. If unlocked_badges.length > 0: display badges in a flex-row flex-wrap gap-3 grid. Each badge: rounded-2xl bg-white p-4 shadow-sm items-center with badge-specific emoji (streak_3: '🔥3', streak_7: '🔥7', streak_14: '🔥14', streak_30: '👑', total_10: '📊', total_50: '⭐', total_100: '💎') + badge name below. If no badges: empty state with lock emoji + 'Keep checking in to unlock badges!'

5. Milestones section (mt-6 px-6): 'Next Milestones' header. Show progress toward next unearned badge. E.g., if current_streak is 5, show 'streak_7' with progress bar at 5/7 (71%).

File: mobile/app/(protected)/_layout.tsx — Replace the 'settings' Tabs.Screen with 'profile' (title: 'Profile', icon: 'person-outline'). Keep settings screen but remove it from tabs (href: null, like paywall).

File: mobile/app/(protected)/settings.tsx — No changes needed, just hidden from tab bar.

This gives users a dedicated place to see their progress and feel motivated by badge achievements.

tasks.title: Add daily reminder notification system with customizable time
tasks.description:
The Settings screen shows 'Coming Soon' for notifications. Implement a real daily reminder system using expo-notifications (already compatible with Expo SDK 54).

File: mobile/lib/notifications.ts — Create new utility:

Imports: * as Notifications from 'expo-notifications', Platform.

Functions:
1. `requestNotificationPermissions(): Promise<boolean>` — Request permissions, return granted status.
2. `scheduleDailyReminder(hour: number, minute: number): Promise<string>` — Cancel existing 'daily-reminder' notifications, then schedule a new daily trigger: `{ hour, minute, repeats: true }` with content: `{ title: 'Time to Check In 💜', body: 'How are you feeling today? Take a moment to log your vibes.', sound: true }`. Return the notification identifier.
3. `cancelDailyReminder(): Promise<void>` — Cancel all scheduled notifications with identifier containing 'daily-reminder'.
4. `getScheduledReminder(): Promise<Notifications.NotificationTrigger | null>` — Get the current scheduled reminder trigger if any.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/settings.tsx — Replace the 'Coming Soon' notification section:

1. Add state: reminderEnabled (boolean), reminderHour (number, default 20), reminderMinute (number, default 0).
2. On mount: Check if a reminder is scheduled via getScheduledReminder(). If exists, set reminderEnabled=true and extract hour/minute.
3. Add Switch for 'Daily Reminder' toggle. When enabled: request permissions, if granted schedule reminder at selected time. When disabled: cancel reminder.
4. When enabled, show time picker: Row of Pressable buttons for common times (9 AM, 12 PM, 6 PM, 8 PM, 10 PM). Selected time shows in primary-600. Tapping a time reschedules the reminder.
5. Below toggle: Text showing 'Reminder set for 8:00 PM daily' in text-sm text-gray-500.

Add `expo-notifications` to package.json dependencies: Run `npx expo install expo-notifications` equivalent by adding to package.json.

Note: Since we cannot run npm install during the test, just add the import and ensure TypeScript types are correct. The test command only checks types.

tasks.title: Add mood journal with rich text notes and photo attachment support
tasks.description:
Enhance the check-in experience with an expanded journal feature. Currently notes are limited to a single-line TextInput with 280 chars. Add a dedicated journal screen that opens after the basic check-in, allowing users to write longer reflections.

File: backend/internal/models/feel_check.go — Add JournalEntry field to FeelCheck model: `JournalEntry string \`json:"journal_entry" gorm:"type:text"\``. This allows unlimited text (vs the 280-char note).

File: backend/internal/dto/feel_dto.go — Add `JournalEntry string \`json:"journal_entry"\`` to CreateFeelCheckRequest and FeelCheckResponse.

File: backend/internal/services/feel_service.go — In CreateFeelCheck, save req.JournalEntry to the FeelCheck record.

File: backend/internal/handlers/feel_handler.go — The CreateFeelCheck handler already binds the full request body, so the new field will be picked up automatically.

File: mobile/types/feel.ts — Add `journal_entry?: string` to FeelCheck interface.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/home.tsx — After successful check-in (in handleCheckIn after setTodayCheck), instead of immediately showing the share prompt, show a journal prompt: Alert with 'Add Journal Entry?' / 'Write about your day' / [Skip, Write]. If 'Write', set showJournal=true.

Add journal UI: When showJournal is true, render a modal or expandable section with:
- Header: 'Journal Entry' + date
- Large TextInput: multiline, numberOfLines={8}, placeholder='Write about your day...', bg-gray-50 rounded-2xl p-4 text-base, min-height 200
- Character count display
- 'Save' Button that calls PATCH /feels/{id}/journal (or re-POSTs the updated check-in)
- After save, show share prompt

File: backend/internal/handlers/feel_handler.go — Add UpdateJournalEntry handler:
```go
func (h *FeelHandler) UpdateJournal(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
    // Get check ID from params, verify ownership, update journal_entry field
}
```

File: backend/internal/routes/routes.go — Add route: `feels.Put("/:id/journal", feelHandler.UpdateJournal)`

In the today's check-in card (home.tsx), if todayCheck.journal_entry exists, show it in a styled card below the note with a book emoji icon and the journal text in text-sm text-gray-700 italic.
tasks.test_command: cd backend && go build ./... && cd ../mobile && npx tsc --noEmit

tasks.title: Add custom tab bar with Slot for NativeWind v4 compatibility and gradient accent
tasks.description:
The current protected layout uses expo-router's built-in <Tabs> component which has limited NativeWind styling support. Replace with a custom tab bar using <Slot> for full NativeWind control and add a gradient accent line.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/_layout.tsx — Complete rewrite:

Imports: React, View, Text, Pressable, Platform from react-native, SafeAreaView from react-native-safe-area-context, Slot, usePathname, useRouter from expo-router, Ionicons from @expo/vector-icons, useAuth from contexts, hapticSelection from lib/haptics.

Create TAB_CONFIG array:
```typescript
const TABS = [
  { name: 'home', title: 'Feels', icon: 'heart', activeIcon: 'heart' },
  { name: 'history', title: 'History', icon: 'calendar-outline', activeIcon: 'calendar' },
  { name: 'insights', title: 'Insights', icon: 'analytics-outline', activeIcon: 'analytics' },
  { name: 'friends', title: 'Friends', icon: 'people-outline', activeIcon: 'people' },
  { name: 'profile', title: 'Profile', icon: 'person-outline', activeIcon: 'person' },
];
```

CustomTabBar component:
- View className='bg-white border-t border-gray-100' with paddingBottom for safe area
- Top accent: 3px height View with purple-to-pink gradient effect using primary-500 backgroundColor
- Tab row: flex-row justify-around py-2
- Each tab: Pressable with items-center py-1 px-3. Active tab: icon in primary-600, label in text-xs font-semibold text-primary-600. Inactive: icon in gray-400, label in text-xs text-gray-400.
- Active indicator: small 4px height 4px width rounded-full bg-primary-500 dot below active icon
- onPress: hapticSelection() + router.push(`/(protected)/${tab.name}`)

Main component:
- Auth guard (same redirect logic as current)
- SafeAreaView flex-1
- Slot (renders current screen)
- CustomTabBar at bottom

This approach gives full NativeWind styling control over the tab bar, allows gradient accents, custom animations, and better iOS visual integration. The Slot component from expo-router renders whatever screen matches the current URL.

tasks.title: Add animated onboarding with Lottie-style emoji animations and progress indicator
tasks.description:
The current onboarding is a basic horizontal ScrollView with static emoji and text. Enhance it with smooth animations and a compelling visual experience.

File: mobile/app/onboarding.tsx — Enhanced rewrite:

Keep the same 3-page structure but enhance each page with animated elements using react-native-reanimated (already in package.json):

1. Import Animated, useSharedValue, useAnimatedStyle, withSpring, withTiming, FadeIn, FadeInUp, FadeInDown, SlideInRight from 'react-native-reanimated'.

2. Page 1 ('Welcome to Feelsy'): 
   - Large pulsing heart emoji: use useAnimatedStyle with a loop animation that scales from 1.0 to 1.15 and back
   - Title fades in with FadeInUp delay 200
   - Subtitle fades in with FadeInUp delay 400
   - Background: subtle primary-50 gradient feel using a large rounded View with primary-100 bg behind the emoji

3. Page 2 ('Understand Your Feelings'):
   - Show 3 mini mood cards that slide in sequentially (FadeInRight with staggered delays 200, 400, 600)
   - Each card: rounded-2xl bg-white shadow-sm p-3, containing emoji + score + small colored bar
   - Cards: 😊 85 (green), 😌 62 (lime), 😔 35 (orange)
   - Below cards: text explaining 'Track mood, energy, and daily patterns'

4. Page 3 ('Ready to Start?'):
   - Sparkle emoji with rotation animation (rotate 0 to 360 continuously)
   - CTA buttons with spring animation on press (scale down to 0.95 then back)
   - 'Try Free' button: bg-primary-600 text-white rounded-2xl py-4
   - 'Sign In' button: border-2 border-primary-600 text-primary-600 rounded-2xl py-4

5. Replace dot indicators with animated progress bar: 
   - View at bottom, h-1 bg-gray-200 rounded-full mx-16
   - Inner View h-1 bg-primary-600 rounded-full, width animated from 33% to 66% to 100% based on activePage
   - Transition uses withSpring for bouncy feel

6. Add Skip button in top-right corner on pages 0 and 1: Text 'Skip' in text-primary-600 font-medium, onPress navigates to page 3 or directly to handleSignIn.

All animations use react-native-reanimated's Animated components (Animated.View, Animated.Text). No external animation libraries needed since reanimated is already installed.

tasks.title: Add streak celebration animations and milestone notifications on home screen
tasks.description:
When users achieve streak milestones (3, 7, 14, 30 days) or unlock badges, there is no celebration feedback. Add animated celebrations to increase engagement.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/home.tsx

1. Add imports: Animated, useSharedValue, useAnimatedStyle, withSpring, withSequence, withDelay, withTiming, runOnJS from 'react-native-reanimated'.

2. Add state: showCelebration (boolean), celebrationData ({ badge: string, emoji: string, message: string } | null).

3. After successful check-in in handleCheckIn, compare previous stats with new stats. If current_streak crossed a milestone threshold (3, 7, 14, 30) OR if unlocked_badges has new entries compared to previous badges:
   - Set celebrationData with appropriate emoji and message:
     - streak_3: { badge: 'streak_3', emoji: '🔥', message: '3 Day Streak! You\'re building a habit!' }
     - streak_7: { badge: 'streak_7', emoji: '⭐', message: '1 Week Streak! Incredible consistency!' }
     - streak_14: { badge: 'streak_14', emoji: '👑', message: '2 Week Streak! You\'re unstoppable!' }
     - streak_30: { badge: 'streak_30', emoji: '💎', message: '30 Day Streak! Legendary!' }
   - Set showCelebration = true

4. Celebration overlay component (rendered when showCelebration is true):
   - Full screen absolute positioned View with bg-black/50 (semi-transparent overlay)
   - Center card: Animated.View with rounded-3xl bg-white p-8 mx-8 shadow-lg
   - Entry animation: scale from 0 to 1.1 to 1.0 using withSequence(withSpring)
   - Large emoji text-7xl at top with bounce animation
   - Badge name in text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900
   - Message in text-base text-gray-600 text-center mt-3
   - Confetti effect: 8-12 small colored circles (8x8 rounded-full) positioned absolutely around the card, each with different withDelay + translateY animation falling from top
   - Colors for confetti: primary-400, primary-600, green-400, yellow-400, pink-400
   - 'Awesome!' Button at bottom, onPress: setShowCelebration(false), hapticSuccess()
   - Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds with setTimeout

5. Store previous badges in a ref: `const prevBadgesRef = useRef<string[]>([])`. Update after each loadData(). Compare in handleCheckIn to detect new badges.

This creates a dopamine-reward loop that encourages daily check-ins and makes badge unlocks feel genuinely exciting.

tasks.title: Add weekly mood recap push notification with score summary
tasks.description:
Add a backend endpoint that generates a weekly recap summary, and expose it for the mobile app to display as a rich notification-style card every Monday.

File: backend/internal/handlers/feel_handler.go — Add GetWeeklyRecap handler:
```go
func (h *FeelHandler) GetWeeklyRecap(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
    userID := extractUserID(c)
    recap, err := h.service.GetWeeklyRecap(userID)
    if err != nil {
        return c.Status(404).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": true, "message": "No data for recap"})
    }
    return c.JSON(recap)
}
```

File: backend/internal/services/feel_service.go — Add GetWeeklyRecap method:
```go
func (s *FeelService) GetWeeklyRecap(userID uuid.UUID) (*dto.WeeklyRecapResponse, error)
```
Logic: Query last 7 days of check-ins. Calculate: total check-ins, average feel score, best score + day, mood trend (array of 7 scores for mini chart), top emoji (most used), streak status. Return structured response.

File: backend/internal/dto/feel_dto.go — Add WeeklyRecapResponse:
```go
type WeeklyRecapResponse struct {
    TotalCheckins int       `json:"total_checkins"`
    AverageScore  float64   `json:"average_score"`
    BestScore     int       `json:"best_score"`
    BestDay       string    `json:"best_day"`
    TopEmoji      string    `json:"top_emoji"`
    DailyScores   []int     `json:"daily_scores"`
    CurrentStreak int       `json:"current_streak"`
    WeekStart     string    `json:"week_start"`
    WeekEnd       string    `json:"week_end"`
}
```

File: backend/internal/routes/routes.go — Add route: `feels.Get("/recap", feelHandler.GetWeeklyRecap)`

File: mobile/app/(protected)/home.tsx — After loading today's data, also call GET /feels/recap. If it's Monday (new Date().getDay() === 1) and user hasn't dismissed it today (check SecureStore 'last_recap_dismissed' date), show a recap card at the top of the home screen:
- Rounded-3xl bg-primary-50 p-6 border border-primary-200
- Header: 'Your Week in Review' + top emoji
- Mini bar chart: 7 small bars (each 24px wide, height proportional to score, bg-primary-500 rounded-t-lg)
- Stats row: avg score, best day, streak
- 'Dismiss' pressable in top-right corner, saves today's date to SecureStore

This adds a Monday ritual that pulls users back to review their week.

tasks.title: Add accessibility labels and VoiceOver support to all interactive elements
tasks.description:
The app is missing accessibility labels on all interactive elements, which could cause App Store rejection under accessibility guidelines and excludes visually impaired users.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/home.tsx — Add accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole to:
- Check-in prompt Pressable: accessibilityLabel='Start daily mood check-in' accessibilityRole='button'

- Log My Feels button: already has title prop, add accessibilityHint='Submits your daily mood check-in'

- History cards: accessibilityLabel={`Check-in on ${date}, score ${score}, ${label}`} accessibilityRole='summary'

File: mobile/app/(protected)/insights.tsx — Add to:

File: mobile/app/onboarding.tsx — Add to:

- Dot indicators: accessibilityLabel={`Page ${index + 1} of 3`} accessibilityState={{ selected: index === activePage }}
- Try Free button: accessibilityHint='Start using Feelsy without an account'

File: mobile/components/ui/Button.tsx — Add accessibilityRole='button' and accessibilityState={{ disabled: isLoading }} to the Pressable wrapper.

This ensures full VoiceOver compatibility on iOS, improving the app experience for all users.

tasks.title: Add error boundary component and offline detection with graceful fallbacks
tasks.description:
The app has no error boundary (crashes show white screen) and no offline handling (API calls fail silently). Add both for production robustness.

File: mobile/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx — Create class component:

Imports: React, Component, View, Text, Pressable from react-native, SafeAreaView from react-native-safe-area-context.

Props: { children: React.ReactNode }
State: { hasError: boolean, error: Error | null }

static getDerivedStateFromError(error): return { hasError: true, error }
componentDidCatch(error, info): console.error('App Error:', error, info)

render(): If hasError, show error UI:
- SafeAreaView className='flex-1 bg-white items-center justify-center px-8'
- Text '😰' text-6xl
- Text 'Something went wrong' text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mt-6 text-center
- Text error.message in text-sm text-gray-500 mt-2 text-center
- Pressable (restart button): rounded-2xl bg-primary-600 px-8 py-4 mt-8, onPress: this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null })
- Text 'Try Again' text-white text-lg font-semibold

File: mobile/lib/network.ts — Create network status utility:

Imports: useState, useEffect from react, AppState from react-native, api from './api'.

Export hook `useNetworkStatus()`: Returns { isOnline: boolean, isChecking: boolean }.
- On mount: Ping GET /health. If success, isOnline=true. If error, isOnline=false.
- Listen to AppState changes: When app comes to foreground, re-check connectivity.
- Re-check every 30 seconds when offline.

File: mobile/components/OfflineBanner.tsx — Create component:
- Uses useNetworkStatus hook
- When offline: Animated.View that slides down from top, bg-red-500 py-2 px-4
- Text 'No internet connection' text-white text-sm font-medium text-center
- Ionicons 'cloud-offline-outline' icon

File: mobile/app/_layout.tsx — Wrap children with ErrorBoundary component. Add OfflineBanner inside the provider tree but above the Slot/navigation.

File: mobile/app/(protected)/home.tsx — When offline (from useNetworkStatus), show cached today check-in if available in AsyncStorage. Before each API call, check isOnline. If offline, show Alert('You are offline. Your check-in will be saved when you reconnect.') and store pending check-in in AsyncStorage under 'pending_checkin'. On next loadData when online, check for pending_checkin and submit it first.

This ensures the app never shows a white screen on crash and gracefully handles connectivity issues.
